title: Bible Quiz: Genesis
description: Creation, the flood, and the patriarchs.

questions.text: In how many days did God create the heavens and the earth, resting on the seventh?
Who were the first man and woman created by God?
How many days and nights did it rain during Noah's flood?
What was the name of Abraham's nephew who lived in Sodom?
How many sons did Jacob have, whose names became the twelve tribes of Israel?

title: Bible Quiz: Exodus
description: Moses, the plagues, and the Ten Commandments.

questions.text: Who led the Israelites out of slavery in Egypt?
How many plagues did God send upon Egypt?
What body of water did the Israelites cross as God parted it?
On what mountain did Moses receive the Ten Commandments?
What was the name of Moses' brother who spoke on his behalf to Pharaoh?

title: Bible Quiz: Leviticus
description: Priestly law, sacrifice, and holiness.

questions.text: Leviticus is primarily addressed to which tribe, concerning priestly duties?
What annual day involved a solemn atonement for the sins of Israel?
Leviticus 19 contains the command to love your neighbor as...?
What animal, sent into the wilderness, symbolically carried away Israel's sins?
Leviticus prescribes a rest for the land every how many years, called a Sabbath year?

title: Bible Quiz: Numbers
description: The wilderness wanderings and the census.

questions.text: Numbers gets its name from the census counting what?
How many spies were sent to scout the Promised Land?
How many of the spies gave a good, faithful report about the land?
How many years did the Israelites wander in the wilderness?
What did God use to speak through a stubborn prophet's donkey?

title: Bible Quiz: Deuteronomy
description: Moses' final words to Israel.

questions.text: Deuteronomy consists mainly of Moses' final speeches to whom?
What famous command begins 'Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God is one Lord'?
Who succeeded Moses as leader of Israel, as recorded near the end of Deuteronomy?
From what mountain did Moses view the Promised Land before he died?
Deuteronomy repeats and expands on which earlier set of laws?

title: Bible Quiz: Joshua
description: Entering and conquering the Promised Land.

questions.text: Who led Israel into the Promised Land after Moses died?
What city's walls famously fell after the Israelites marched around it?
What did the Israelites carry as they marched around the city for seven days?
Which spy/prostitute in Jericho hid the Israelite spies and was spared?
Joshua divided the conquered land among how many tribes of Israel?

title: Bible Quiz: Judges
description: Israel's cycle of sin and deliverance.

questions.text: What was the role of a 'judge' in the book of Judges?

options.t: A deliverer/leader raised up by God

questions.text: Which judge defeated the Midianites with only 300 men?
Which judge's strength was tied to his uncut hair?
Who deceived Samson and had his hair cut off?
Which female judge and prophetess led Israel to victory over Canaan?

title: Bible Quiz: Ruth
description: Loyalty, redemption, and a Moabite widow.

questions.text: What nationality was Ruth before joining Israel?
What was the name of Ruth's mother-in-law?
What field did Ruth glean grain in, owned by her future husband?
What is the term for the family duty Boaz fulfilled by marrying Ruth?
Ruth became the great-grandmother of which famous Israelite king?

title: Bible Quiz: 1 Samuel
description: Samuel, Saul, and the rise of David.

questions.text: Who was Samuel's mother, who prayed fervently for a son?
Who was anointed as Israel's first king?
Which giant Philistine warrior did David famously defeat?
What weapon did David use to defeat the giant?
Who was Saul's son and David's close friend?

title: Bible Quiz: 2 Samuel
description: King David's reign and its troubles.

questions.text: Which tribe did David first reign over before becoming king of all Israel?
What city did David capture and make his capital?
With whom did David commit adultery, leading to a major moral fall?
Which of David's sons led a rebellion against him?
Which prophet confronted David over his sin with Bathsheba?

title: Bible Quiz: 1 Kings
description: Solomon's wisdom and the divided kingdom.

questions.text: Which king asked God for wisdom instead of riches?
What famous building did Solomon construct in Jerusalem?
After Solomon's death, the kingdom split into Israel and what other kingdom?
Which prophet challenged the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel?
Which queen famously visited Solomon to test his wisdom?

title: Bible Quiz: 2 Kings
description: The fall of Israel and Judah.

questions.text: Which prophet succeeded Elijah and received a double portion of his spirit?
How was Elijah taken up to heaven?

options.t: In a chariot of fire in a whirlwind
He simply died of old age

questions.text: Which empire conquered the northern kingdom of Israel?
Which empire eventually destroyed Jerusalem and its Temple?
Which righteous king of Judah instituted major religious reforms after finding the Book of the Law?

title: Bible Quiz: 1 Chronicles
description: Genealogies and David's preparations for the Temple.

questions.text: 1 Chronicles opens with long lists of what?
Which king is the central focus of 1 Chronicles?
What did David gather materials and plans for, though he did not build it himself?
Who was chosen to actually build the Temple instead of David?
1 Chronicles organizes worship duties among which tribe?

title: Bible Quiz: 2 Chronicles
description: Solomon's Temple through the exile.

questions.text: 2 Chronicles opens with the reign of which king building the Temple?
2 Chronicles focuses mainly on the kings of which kingdom, unlike Kings which covers both?
Which reforming king of Judah tore down idols and celebrated a great Passover?
2 Chronicles ends with the decree of which Persian king allowing the Jews to return home?
What disaster closes out the book, marking the end of the kingdom of Judah?

title: Bible Quiz: Ezra
description: Rebuilding the Temple after exile.

questions.text: Ezra describes the Jews' return from exile in which empire's territory?
Which Persian king first decreed that the Jews could return and rebuild the Temple?
What was Ezra's profession/role among the returning exiles?
What structure was the primary focus of rebuilding in the book of Ezra?
What issue among the returned exiles did Ezra confront regarding intermarriage?

options.t: Marrying foreign wives who worshipped other gods

title: Bible Quiz: Nehemiah
description: Rebuilding Jerusalem's walls.

questions.text: What was Nehemiah's job before returning to Jerusalem?
What did Nehemiah lead the people to rebuild?
How long did it take to complete the rebuilding project, famously fast?
Who were the main opponents that mocked and tried to stop Nehemiah's work?
Nehemiah served the people in what official capacity in Jerusalem?

title: Bible Quiz: Esther
description: A queen who saved her people.

questions.text: Esther became queen of which empire?
Who was Esther's cousin and guardian who raised her?
Which villain plotted to destroy the Jewish people in the story?
What Jewish festival celebrates the deliverance recorded in Esther?
What is notable about the book of Esther compared to most other Bible books?

options.t: It never directly mentions God's name
It is written entirely in poetry
It is the shortest book in the Bible

title: Bible Quiz: Job
description: Suffering, faith, and God's sovereignty.

questions.text: Job is described in his book's opening as a man who was what?
Who challenged God that Job only served Him because of his blessings?
How many friends came to comfort (and later argue with) Job?
Out of what did God finally speak to Job near the end of the book?
How did Job's fortunes end at the close of the book?

title: Bible Quiz: Psalms
description: Songs and prayers of ancient Israel.

questions.text: How many psalms are there in the book of Psalms?
Which king is credited with writing the most psalms?
Psalm 23 famously begins 'The Lord is my...' what?
What term describes psalms that express sorrow or a cry for help?
The final psalm (150) calls for praising God using what kind of instruments?

title: Bible Quiz: Proverbs
description: Wisdom for everyday living.

questions.text: Which king is credited as the primary author of Proverbs?
Proverbs says the fear of the Lord is the beginning of what?
Proverbs 31 famously describes what kind of person?
Proverbs frequently contrasts wisdom with what opposite?
What literary style makes up most of the book of Proverbs?

title: Bible Quiz: Ecclesiastes
description: The search for meaning under the sun.

questions.text: Ecclesiastes' narrator calls himself 'the Teacher' or by what other title?
What repeated phrase describes life's fleeting nature in Ecclesiastes?
Ecclesiastes 3 famously says there is 'a time for every...' what?
The book is traditionally attributed to which wise king?
Ecclesiastes concludes that the whole duty of humanity is to do what?

options.t: Fear God and keep His commandments

title: Bible Quiz: Song of Solomon
description: A poetic celebration of love.

questions.text: Song of Solomon is primarily written in what literary form?
The book is traditionally attributed to which king, as its title suggests?
Song of Solomon is also sometimes called by what other name?
What is a notable feature of this book compared to most of the Old Testament?

options.t: It does not mention God's name directly
It is written entirely in Greek
It is a set of legal rulings

questions.text: The main speakers in the poetic dialogue are a bridegroom and who else?

title: Bible Quiz: Isaiah
description: Judgment, comfort, and the coming Messiah.

questions.text: Isaiah prophesied primarily to which kingdom?
Isaiah 53 is a famous prophecy describing what figure who suffers for others?
What famous title does Isaiah give a coming child, 'Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father,' and what else?
Isaiah saw a vision of God's throne room featuring what six-winged creatures?
Isaiah is often divided by scholars into sections dealing with judgment and what other theme?

title: Bible Quiz: Jeremiah
description: The weeping prophet warns of Jerusalem's fall.

questions.text: Jeremiah is often nicknamed what, due to his sorrowful message?
Jeremiah warned Judah of coming conquest by which empire?
What object did Jeremiah famously smash to symbolize God's judgment?
Jeremiah spent time thrown into what for his unpopular prophecies?
Jeremiah spoke of a coming 'new' what, which God would write on people's hearts?

title: Bible Quiz: Lamentations
description: Mourning the destruction of Jerusalem.

questions.text: Lamentations mourns the destruction of which city?
Lamentations is traditionally attributed to which prophet?
What literary form structures most chapters of Lamentations?
Despite its sorrow, Lamentations 3 contains a famous statement about God's what being new every morning?
Lamentations follows chronologically right after the events of which book?

title: Bible Quiz: Ezekiel
description: Visions of judgment, exile, and restoration.

questions.text: Ezekiel prophesied mainly to Jewish exiles living in which empire?
Ezekiel's famous vision in chapter 37 involves a valley full of what?
What happened to the dry bones after Ezekiel prophesied to them?

options.t: They came together and lived again

questions.text: Ezekiel's opening vision describes strange four-faced creatures alongside what fiery object?
Ezekiel is often called by what title emphasizing his role as a warner?

title: Bible Quiz: Daniel
description: Faith in exile and visions of the future.

questions.text: Daniel and his friends were taken as young captives to which empire?
What den was Daniel thrown into for continuing to pray?
Which three of Daniel's friends were thrown into a fiery furnace for refusing to worship an idol?

options.t: Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah's Greek names only

questions.text: Daniel interpreted a king's dream of a large statue made of what materials?

options.t: Gold, silver, bronze, iron, and clay

questions.text: What mysterious writing appeared on the wall during King Belshazzar's feast?

title: Bible Quiz: Hosea
description: God's faithful love for an unfaithful Israel.

questions.text: Hosea's own marriage was used as a picture of God's relationship with whom?
What was unusual about the woman Hosea was told to marry?
What is the name of Hosea's wife?
Hosea's message centers on God's steadfast what, despite Israel's unfaithfulness?
Hosea prophesied primarily to which kingdom, shortly before its fall?

title: Bible Quiz: Joel
description: A locust plague and the promise of God's Spirit.

questions.text: What disaster does Joel use as an image of coming judgment?
Joel's prophecy about God pouring out His Spirit 'on all flesh' is famously quoted in which New Testament event?
Joel calls the people to respond to disaster with fasting and what else?
What day of judgment does Joel repeatedly warn is near?
Joel promises restoration of what after the locusts had devastated the land?

title: Bible Quiz: Amos
description: A shepherd calls for justice.

questions.text: What was Amos's occupation before becoming a prophet?
Amos prophesied mainly against which kingdom's social injustice?
Amos famously calls for justice to 'roll down like' what?
Amos criticized Israel's wealthy for oppressing whom?
Amos used a plumb line as a symbol of what?

title: Bible Quiz: Obadiah
description: Judgment against Edom, the shortest Old Testament book.

questions.text: Obadiah is the shortest book in which testament?

options.t: Neither, it is the shortest overall

questions.text: Obadiah's prophecy is directed against which nation, descended from Esau?
Edom is condemned for its treatment of which related nation during its distress?
Edom's ancestor, Esau, was the brother and rival of which patriarch?
Obadiah declares that 'the day of the Lord' is near for all what?

title: Bible Quiz: Jonah
description: A reluctant prophet and a great fish.

questions.text: To what city was Jonah told to go and preach repentance?
Where did Jonah try to flee to instead of obeying God?
What swallowed Jonah after he was thrown overboard?
How many days and nights was Jonah inside the fish?
How did the city respond when Jonah finally preached there?

options.t: They repented in sackcloth and ashes

title: Bible Quiz: Micah
description: Justice, mercy, and a promised ruler from Bethlehem.

questions.text: Micah prophesied that a future ruler would be born in which small town?
Micah 6:8 famously summarizes God's requirement as doing justice, loving mercy, and what else?
Like Isaiah, Micah prophesied primarily concerning which kingdom?
Micah condemned corrupt leaders for oppressing whom?
Micah pictures swords being beaten into what peaceful tool, echoing Isaiah?

title: Bible Quiz: Nahum
description: Judgment against Nineveh.

questions.text: Nahum's prophecy announces judgment on which city, previously spared in Jonah's time?
Nineveh was the capital of which empire?
Nahum describes God as slow to anger but great in what?

options.t: Power, and He will not leave the guilty unpunished

questions.text: Nahum's message serves as a sequel of sorts to which earlier prophetic book set in the same city?
Nahum is largely a poetic description of the coming military what?

title: Bible Quiz: Habakkuk
description: A prophet questions God and learns to trust.

questions.text: Habakkuk's book is structured mainly as a dialogue between the prophet and whom?
Habakkuk questions why God would use which violent nation to punish Judah?
Habakkuk's famous line 'the righteous shall live by his ___' is later quoted heavily in the New Testament.
The book ends with Habakkuk expressing what, despite hardship?
Habakkuk 2 instructs that a vision be written on what, so a runner could read it?

title: Bible Quiz: Zephaniah
description: The great Day of the Lord.

questions.text: Zephaniah prophesied during the reign of which reforming king of Judah?
Zephaniah's central theme is the coming of what significant day?
Despite warnings of judgment, Zephaniah ends with promises of what for a faithful remnant?
Zephaniah warns judgment will fall on Judah and also on surrounding what?
Zephaniah 3 describes God rejoicing over His people with what?

title: Bible Quiz: Haggai
description: A call to rebuild the Temple.

questions.text: Haggai urged the returned exiles to finish rebuilding what?
Haggai prophesied alongside which other post-exilic prophet?
Haggai rebuked the people for living in nice houses while what remained in ruins?
Under which Persian king's reign did Haggai's prophecies take place?
Haggai encouraged which governor, alongside the high priest, to lead the rebuilding?

title: Bible Quiz: Zechariah
description: Visions of a restored Jerusalem and a coming king.

questions.text: Zechariah prophesied to encourage the rebuilding of what, alongside Haggai?
Zechariah is filled with many strange and symbolic what?
Zechariah prophesied a king coming to Jerusalem humbly riding on what animal, later fulfilled by Jesus?
Zechariah prophesied about thirty pieces of what being paid as a price, later echoed in Jesus' betrayal?
Zechariah's visions include a high priest named Joshua and a governor named whom?

title: Bible Quiz: Malachi
description: The last words of the Old Testament.

questions.text: Malachi is the final book of which section of the Bible?
Malachi rebukes the priests for offering God what kind of unacceptable sacrifices?
Malachi accuses the people of robbing God by withholding what?
Malachi ends with a promise that God would send a prophet like whom before 'the great and dreadful day of the Lord'?
After Malachi, the Old Testament ends, followed after centuries of silence by which New Testament book?

title: Bible Quiz: Matthew
description: Jesus as the promised Jewish Messiah.

questions.text: Matthew's Gospel opens with what, tracing Jesus' ancestry?
Who visited the infant Jesus bringing gold, frankincense, and myrrh?
Matthew 5-7 contains Jesus' famous collection of teachings known as what?
Which king tried to kill the infant Jesus by ordering the death of young boys in Bethlehem?
Matthew's Gospel ends with Jesus giving what commission to His disciples?

title: Bible Quiz: Mark
description: Jesus as the servant, fast-paced and action-focused.

questions.text: Mark's Gospel is generally considered the shortest and most what in style?
Mark's Gospel is traditionally linked to the preaching of which apostle?
Mark frequently uses which word, translated 'immediately,' to move the story quickly?
Mark's Gospel presents Jesus primarily in what role, unlike Matthew's kingly focus?
Mark records Jesus calming what natural event on the Sea of Galilee?

title: Bible Quiz: Luke
description: Jesus as the compassionate Son of Man.

questions.text: Luke's Gospel was written by a companion of which apostle?
Luke uniquely records the parable of which forgiving father and rebellious son?
Luke's Gospel places special emphasis on Jesus' compassion toward whom?
Luke uniquely records the parable of which compassionate traveler helping an injured stranger?
Luke is also the author of which other New Testament book, a sequel of sorts?

title: Bible Quiz: John
description: Jesus as the divine Son of God.

questions.text: John's Gospel opens with a poetic prologue calling Jesus what?
John records seven significant miracles, which he calls by what term?
What was Jesus' first miracle recorded in John's Gospel, at a wedding?
John 3:16 is one of the most quoted verses, describing God's love expressed through what?
John uses several 'I am' statements of Jesus, including 'I am the bread of life' and what other famous one?

title: Bible Quiz: Acts
description: The early church and the spread of the gospel.

questions.text: Acts describes the coming of the Holy Spirit on what day, empowering the early church?
Who was dramatically converted on the road to Damascus, becoming a key missionary?
Who was the first Christian martyr recorded in Acts, stoned to death?
Acts records Paul undertaking several journeys for what purpose?
Acts is written by the same author as which Gospel, as a continuation?

title: Bible Quiz: Romans
description: Paul's letter on sin, grace, and salvation by faith.

questions.text: Romans is a letter written by Paul to the church in which city?
Romans 3:23 famously states that all have sinned and fall short of what?
Romans teaches that salvation comes through faith and not through what?
Romans 8 famously declares that nothing can separate believers from what?
Romans is considered one of Paul's most systematic explanations of what subject?

title: Bible Quiz: 1 Corinthians
description: Correcting division and disorder in the church.

questions.text: 1 Corinthians addresses divisions in the church over what issue?
1 Corinthians 13 is famously known as the chapter about what?
Paul addresses the proper use of spiritual gifts, including speaking in what?
1 Corinthians 15 gives a detailed defense of what key Christian doctrine?
Paul describes the church as one body with many what?

title: Bible Quiz: 2 Corinthians
description: Paul defends his ministry and suffering.

questions.text: 2 Corinthians is notably personal, with Paul defending his what?
Paul describes a mysterious 'thorn in the flesh' given to keep him from what?
Paul teaches that God's power is made perfect in human what?
2 Corinthians 9 teaches that God loves a giver who gives in what manner?
Paul lists numerous sufferings he endured, including shipwrecks and what else?

title: Bible Quiz: Galatians
description: Freedom in Christ versus the Law.

questions.text: Galatians strongly argues that justification comes through faith, not through what?
Galatians 5 lists the 'fruit of the Spirit,' beginning with love, joy, and what?
Paul says in Galatians that believers are called to what kind of freedom?

options.t: Freedom in Christ, not for sin but for love

questions.text: Paul confronted which apostle publicly over hypocrisy regarding Gentile believers?
Galatians teaches that in Christ there is no distinction between Jew and Greek, slave and free, and what other pair?

title: Bible Quiz: Ephesians
description: Unity in Christ and the armor of God.

questions.text: Ephesians teaches that believers are saved by grace through what, not by works?
Ephesians 6 describes believers putting on what for spiritual battle?
Ephesians emphasizes the unity of Jews and Gentiles into what single entity?
Ephesians describes Christ as the head of what, using a body metaphor?
Paul wrote Ephesians, like several other epistles, from where?

title: Bible Quiz: Philippians
description: Joy and contentment despite imprisonment.

questions.text: Philippians is often called Paul's letter of what emotion, despite being written in prison?
Philippians 2 contains a famous hymn describing Christ's what, from equality with God to a servant's death?
Paul says he has learned the secret of being content in what kind of circumstances?

options.t: Any and every circumstance, plenty or want

questions.text: Philippians 4:13 says believers can do all things through whom who strengthens them?
Philippians was written to a church in which Roman colony city, Paul's first European convert location?

title: Bible Quiz: Colossians
description: The supremacy of Christ over all things.

questions.text: Colossians emphasizes Christ's supremacy over what?
Colossians warns against being taken captive by hollow philosophy and what else?
Colossians describes Christ as the 'image of the invisible' whom?
Paul wrote Colossians alongside a shorter personal letter to whom, found right after it in the Bible?
Colossians instructs believers to set their minds on things above, not on what?

title: Bible Quiz: 1 Thessalonians
description: Encouragement and teaching on Christ's return.

questions.text: 1 Thessalonians is considered one of Paul's earliest letters, written to encourage a young what?
1 Thessalonians 4 gives comfort about believers who have died, describing a future what?
Paul instructs believers to 'pray without' doing what?
Paul describes the day of the Lord as coming like what unexpected thing?
Paul commends the Thessalonians for their work of faith and labor of what?

title: Bible Quiz: 2 Thessalonians
description: Clarifying confusion about Christ's return.

questions.text: 2 Thessalonians corrects confusion about what event some believed had already happened?
Paul warns of a coming 'man of lawlessness' before what event?
Paul instructs that those unwilling to work should not do what?
2 Thessalonians encourages believers to stand firm and hold to what they were taught?

options.t: The traditions/teachings passed on by Paul

questions.text: Like 1 Thessalonians, this letter was addressed to believers in which city?

title: Bible Quiz: 1 Timothy
description: Guidance for a young church leader.

questions.text: 1 Timothy is a personal letter from Paul to a young what he had mentored?
1 Timothy gives qualifications for which two church offices?
Paul calls the love of what 'a root of all kinds of evil'?
Paul describes himself as once being the 'chief' or foremost of what?
1 Timothy urges prayers to be made for kings and all who hold what?

title: Bible Quiz: 2 Timothy
description: Paul's final letter before his death.

questions.text: 2 Timothy is widely considered Paul's what, written shortly before his death?
Paul famously says he has fought the good fight and finished what?
Paul reminds Timothy that all Scripture is God-breathed and useful for what?
Paul encourages Timothy not to be ashamed of what, despite his imprisonment?
Paul urges Timothy to be strong in the grace found in whom?

title: Bible Quiz: Titus
description: Instructions for church order in Crete.

questions.text: Titus was left by Paul to organize the church on which island?
Like 1 Timothy, Titus gives qualifications for appointing what church role?
Titus emphasizes that believers should be devoted to doing what?
Paul reminds Titus that believers were saved not by righteous deeds but by God's what?
Titus is grouped with 1 and 2 Timothy as what type of letters, addressed to church leaders?

title: Bible Quiz: Philemon
description: A personal plea for a runaway slave.

questions.text: Philemon is a short personal letter from Paul appealing on behalf of whom?
Paul asks Philemon to receive Onesimus back not as a slave but as what?
Philemon is notable for being one of Paul's most what kind of letters?
Paul offers to personally repay any debt Onesimus owed to whom?
Philemon, like Colossians, was written while Paul was in what situation?

title: Bible Quiz: Hebrews
description: Christ as the superior high priest.

questions.text: Hebrews argues that Christ is superior to angels, Moses, and what religious role?
Hebrews 11 is often called the 'hall of' what, listing examples of trust in God?
Hebrews describes Jesus as a priest forever in the order of whom?
Hebrews teaches that Christ's sacrifice was made once for all, unlike the repeated what?

options.t: Animal sacrifices under the old covenant

questions.text: Hebrews describes faith as being sure of what we hope for and certain of what?

title: Bible Quiz: James
description: Practical faith shown through works.

questions.text: James famously teaches that faith without what is dead?
James compares an uncontrolled tongue to a small spark that can start what?
James is traditionally believed to have been written by Jesus' what?
James instructs believers to consider it pure joy when facing what?
James warns against showing what toward the rich over the poor in the assembly?

title: Bible Quiz: 1 Peter
description: Hope and perseverance through suffering.

questions.text: 1 Peter was written to encourage believers facing what kind of experience?
Peter describes believers as living stones being built into what?
Peter calls believers a chosen people, a royal what, and a holy nation?
Peter urges wives and husbands toward mutual what within marriage?
Peter encourages believers to cast all their anxiety on God because He does what?

title: Bible Quiz: 2 Peter
description: A warning against false teachers.

questions.text: 2 Peter strongly warns the church about the danger of what kind of people?
Peter reminds readers that prophecy did not come from human will but from men speaking from whom?
2 Peter 3 addresses scoffers who doubt the promise of what future event?
Peter states that with the Lord a day is like a thousand years, and vice versa, to illustrate what?
2 Peter closes by urging growth in grace and the knowledge of whom?

options.t: Our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ

title: Bible Quiz: 1 John
description: Assurance, love, and walking in the light.

questions.text: 1 John repeatedly emphasizes that 'God is' what, as a core description?
1 John teaches that if we confess our sins, God is faithful and just to do what?
1 John warns believers to test the spirits because many what have gone into the world?
1 John states that perfect love drives out what?
1 John is traditionally attributed to the same author as which Gospel?

title: Bible Quiz: 2 John
description: A brief warning about deceivers.

questions.text: 2 John is addressed to 'the chosen lady' and her what?
2 John warns against welcoming teachers who deny that Jesus came in what way?
2 John, like 1 John, strongly emphasizes walking in truth and what else?
2 John is one of the shortest books in the Bible, fitting on roughly how many original page(s)?
2 John shares its author with which other short epistle right after it?

title: Bible Quiz: 3 John
description: Praise for hospitality, a warning about pride.

questions.text: 3 John is written personally to a man named whom, commended for hospitality?
3 John criticizes a proud church leader named whom, who refused to welcome fellow believers?
3 John is one of the shortest books in the entire what?
3 John commends Gaius for supporting traveling what, sent out for the gospel?
3 John closes by saying the author hopes to see Gaius soon and speak how?

title: Bible Quiz: Jude
description: A short, urgent call to contend for the faith.

questions.text: Jude urges believers to 'contend for' what, against false teachers?

options.t: The faith once for all entrusted to God's people

questions.text: Jude identifies himself as a brother of which other New Testament epistle-writer?
Jude references the archangel Michael disputing with the devil over whose body?
Jude's closing doxology praises God who is able to keep believers from doing what?
Jude is one of the shortest books, positioned right before which final book of the Bible?

title: Bible Quiz: Revelation
description: Visions of the end and Christ's ultimate victory.

questions.text: Revelation was written by John while exiled on which island?
Revelation opens with letters addressed to how many churches?
Revelation describes a great multitude worshiping around a heavenly what?
Revelation famously describes a final battle at a place called what?
Revelation closes the entire Bible with the promise of a new heaven and what else?

title: Bible General Knowledge
description: Broad questions spanning the whole Bible, Genesis to Revelation.

questions.text: How many books are in the Protestant Bible in total?
How many books make up the Old Testament?
How many books make up the New Testament?
What is the first book of the Bible?
What is the last book of the Bible?
Which Gospel is traditionally placed first in the New Testament?
Who is considered the father of many nations and the founder of the Israelite covenant?
Which prophet was swallowed by a great fish?
Who built an ark to survive a great flood?
Who led the Israelites out of Egypt?
Which king was known for his great wisdom and building the first Temple?
Who defeated the giant Goliath?
Who was thrown into a den of lions for praying?
In what town was Jesus born?
How many disciples did Jesus choose as His closest followers?
Who betrayed Jesus for thirty pieces of silver?
Which disciple denied knowing Jesus three times?
Who wrote the largest number of New Testament epistles?
On what day did the Holy Spirit come upon the disciples in Acts?
What is the shortest verse in the Bible, found in John 11?

title: Bible Riddles
description: Figure-it-out riddles drawn from Bible people, places, and events. This session is clearly marked so you know you're solving riddles, not straightforward questions.

questions.text: I was formed from dust and given the breath of life. My rib became my companion. Who am I?
I was the first murderer, marked so no one would kill me, and I killed my own brother. Who am I?
I built a giant boat on dry land while everyone mocked me, and I was right in the end. Who am I?
I wrestled with a stranger all night and walked away with a new name and a limp. Who am I?
My jealous brothers sold me for silver, but I ended up second-in-command of Egypt. Who am I?
I was found in a basket among the reeds and later stood before Pharaoh with a message from God. Who am I?
My walls fell flat after seven days of marching and trumpet blasts. What city am I?
I lost my great strength when my hair was cut, betrayed by the woman I loved. Who am I?
I was a foreign widow who followed my mother-in-law and gleaned in a rich man's field, later becoming a king's ancestor. Who am I?
I was a shepherd boy who became king after defeating a giant with a sling. Who am I?
I asked God for wisdom instead of riches and became famous for a wise ruling between two women over a baby. Who am I?
Ravens fed me by a brook, and later I challenged hundreds of false prophets to a fire-calling contest on a mountain. Who am I?
I was thrown into a den of hungry lions for refusing to stop praying, and walked out unharmed. Who am I?
Three of my friends were thrown into a blazing furnace for refusing to bow to a golden statue, and a fourth figure appeared with them in the flames. Who are we?
I ran from God's call, was swallowed by a great fish, and ended up preaching to the very city I tried to avoid. Who am I?
I was a queen who risked my life to save my people, saying 'if I perish, I perish.' Who am I?
I doubted a resurrection until I touched the wounds myself. Who am I?
I was a tax collector who climbed a tree just to see Jesus pass by. Who am I?
I persecuted the church breathing threats, then was blinded by a light on a road and became one of its greatest missionaries. Who am I?
I am the last book of the Bible, full of visions, seven churches, and a final victory. What book am I?
